Conclude with Naspers, a major South African media conglomerate, to illustrate how publishing and storytelling evolved into large-scale broadcast and digital media businesses.

riddle:
1. Seek the Wikipedia page for the Victorian author whose forename matches that borne by several English kings and whose surname is the same as that of Victorian painter William Powell Frith (the Victorian author); on that page, read how old he was when he died and note that number, then follow the link labelled "Article" to continue the route.

2. There exists a simple transformation to perform next: take the age you recorded in clue 1 and repeatedly add its digits together until only a single digit remains — that final digit is your clue 2.

3. What is the population of the country that hosts the subscription television channel famed for movie channels and major sports broadcasts? Visit the broadcaster’s home-nation article and record the population as a whole number for clue 3.

4. In the tale of a modest Cape Town newspaper that became a global media conglomerate, open that company’s page and write down the date on which it was founded (the founding date as presented there will be your clue 4).

5. (One last arithmetic lock: use only the numeric answers you collected.) Compute (clue 1 + clue 2 + clue 3) modulo 10 and report the result — it must be a single digit between 0 and 9.

metadata.theme: From Victorian print storytelling to modern South African broadcast media — tracing a line from 19th-century author Henry Frith to contemporary media companies and their audience reach.
